ARC-000-RSCH-v1.0.title: Target-Organisation Research: ArcKit as a Service — UK Public-Sector Adopter Landscape
ARC-000-RSCH-v1.0.owner: Mark Craddock (ArcKit as a Service Owner)

controlFields.Project: ArcKit as a Service — Wave 1 cross-project context (Project 000-global)
controlFields.Review Cycle: Annual (refresh each year of the SOBC outlook)
controlFields.Owner: Mark Craddock (ArcKit as a Service Owner)
controlFields.Distribution: Project 001 team, downstream Wave-2 research subagents (RESEARCH / AWS / AZURE / GCP / GOV_REUSE), CCS liaison, GDS, CDDO, MOD Defence Digital
controlFields.UK Central-Civil Department / ALB (DDaT: Mix of bespoke EA wikis (Confluence, SharePoint), commercial EA tooling (LeanIX, Ardoq, MEGA, Bizzdesign) and Office-document templates. Often partial coverage; assurance-evidence quality variable.
controlFields.NHS Trust / NHS England: Limited dedicated EA tooling; Microsoft 365-centric estate; clinical-system focus. Architecture-governance capability typically thin outside national bodies.
controlFields.MOD Command / Defence Digital: Strong existing assurance machinery (JSP 440, JSP 604, MOD Secure by Design via CAAT). Architecture artefacts often classified above the SaaS ceiling.
controlFields.Devolved administration / Local authorit: Variable; smaller teams; heavy reliance on suppliers. Often no dedicated EA tooling.
controlFields.UK SME supplying the public sector: No commercial EA tooling at SME prices. Bespoke / Office-document governance.
controlFields.Identity / SSO: Entra ID, OIDC, SAML 2.0, GOV.UK One Login (citizen), NHS CIS2 (NHS)
controlFields.Procurement route: G-Cloud (primary), NHS frameworks, local-gov frameworks, direct procurement
controlFields.Assurance language: TCoP, GDS Service Standard, NCSC CAF, NCSC Cloud Security Principles, UK GDPR / DPA 2018, PSBAR / WCAG 2.2 AA, NHS DSPT / DTAC
controlFields.AI / model endpoint: Public-internet provider (with no-training assurances and DPIA)
controlFields.GOV.UK Design System adoption expected?: Yes (FR-013) — SaaS UI matches public-sector visual language

ARC-001-DPIA-v1.0.title: Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA): ArcKit as a Service (Managed SaaS)

controlFields.Document Type: Data Protection Impact Assessment (UK GDPR Article 35)
controlFields.Owner: [PENDING] Vendor Data Protection Officer
controlFields.Reviewed By: [PENDING] Service Owner / SIRO-equivalent, Security Lead, Lead Architect
controlFields.Approved By: [PENDING] Service Owner (data controller for vendor processing)
controlFields.Distribution: Project Team, Architecture Team, ICO (on request), CCS liaison
controlFields.Tenant administrator: Full name, work email, organisation, role title, optional photo
controlFields.Tenant user: Full name, work email, role, optional photo, last-login timestamp
controlFields.Authentication metadata: OIDC / SAML claims (subject ID, audience, scope), MFA state, session tokens (short-lived), device fingerprint (rough — IP + user-agent)
controlFields.Audit log records: User ID + tenant ID + action + timestamp + (optionally) before/after state of artefact metadata
controlFields.Billing records: Organisation legal name, billing contact name + email + role, payment-processor reference (e.g., Stripe customer ID), invoice history
controlFields.Usage telemetry: Per-tenant aggregated metrics; per-user anonymised activity counters used to detect abuse / abandonment patterns
controlFields.AI generation context (transient): Prompt content (artefact draft input — user-authored, may incidentally include user names, work emails, project metadata); tenant ID context (technical correlation only)
controlFields.Companies House data (verification check: Public organisation data: company number, name, status, officers (organisation data — not personal data of *our* data subjects)
controlFields.DPIA-001: Cross-tenant exposure of one tenant's user list / artefact metadata to another tenant
controlFields.DPIA-002: AI sub-processor uses user-supplied prompt (which may incidentally include user names) for model training, against tenant DPA
controlFields.DPIA-003: Personal data sent to AI sub-processor in non-UK jurisdiction without adequate safeguards
controlFields.DPIA-004: Audit log retention exceeds purpose — long-term accumulation of user-identifying activity
controlFields.DPIA-005: Tenant administrator misuses audit log to surveil tenant users (work-context surveillance)
controlFields.DPIA-006: Failure to honour SAR within 1 month timeline
controlFields.DPIA-007: Failure to honour erasure right (deletion + verifiable destruction)
controlFields.DPIA-008: AI sub-processor security incident exposes prompt content (which may include data-subject names within artefact metadata)
controlFields.DPIA-009: Rights-of-subject (rectification, restriction, objection, portability) not fully implemented for all categories
controlFields.DPIA-011: Breach not notified to ICO within 72 hours
controlFields.DPIA-012: AI generation output produces hallucinated personal data attributed to data subjects in artefacts
controlFields.DPIA-013: Insider threat: vendor operator accesses tenant content beyond the support scope
controlFields.DPIA-014: Re-identification of data subjects in "anonymised" aggregate telemetry
controlFields.DPIA-015: Companies House outage forces relaxation of SME verification — wrong tier or wrong basis applied

ARC-001-OPS-v1.0.owner: Mark Craddock (Service Owner — until Operations Lead appointed at GA – 30 days)

controlFields.Review Cycle: Quarterly during pre-GA; bi-annual post-GA + 6 months
controlFields.Owner: Mark Craddock (Service Owner — until Operations Lead appointed at GA – 30 days)
controlFields.Distribution: Project Team, SRE, Security, DPO, Project 002 (Sovereign) liaison, Pilot DDaT Architects
controlFields.Service Name: ArcKit as a Service (managed multi-tenant SaaS route, Project 001)
controlFields.Description: Managed SaaS for UK SMEs supplying UK Government — enterprise architecture governance toolkit producing principles, requirements, ADRs, diagrams, business cases, risk and compliance artefacts. Sister project 002 ships the same software as a sovereign / air-gapped bundle to UK Government deploying authorities.
controlFields.Business Criticality: High (livelihood-impact for SME tenants in active bid windows; reputational existential for vendor)
controlFields.Operations Lead / SRE Lead: [PENDING — appointment GA – 30 days; must be in seat for handover §14]
controlFields.DPO: [PENDING — DPO mailbox stood up pre-GA per DPIA §11]
controlFields.Tenancy Stage: Pre-GA Alpha; no live OFFICIAL tenant data yet
controlFields.Export Service Latency p95: 95th percentile time from `POST /exports` to signed URL availability for tenants ≤ 100 MB tarball
controlFields.Tenant Isolation Anomaly Rate: Count of admission-denial events, network-policy denials, RBAC denials suggesting cross-tenant attempts
controlFields.API Latency (p95): < 500 ms for tenant-data CRUD; < 1 s for search
controlFields.DR Recovery Time (RTO): < 4 hours from declared regional outage to service restored on DR site
controlFields.DR Data Loss (RPO): < 15 minutes of writes lost in worst-case regional failure
controlFields.L1 — Vendor support desk: Vendor support (single shared queue across SaaS tenants)
controlFields.L4 — External: Cloud vendor support (per ADR-002), AI provider support (per ADR-004), payment processor, IdP federation provider
controlFields.P1: Service down for ≥ 1 cell, OR confirmed cross-tenant data leakage, OR audit-log integrity breach, OR identity service compromise
controlFields.P2: SLO burning fast OR major feature degraded for ≥ 1 cell OR pen-test critical / high finding live in production OR AI provider outage with no cleared failover
controlFields.P3: Minor degradation, single-tenant impact, deferred fix possible
controlFields.SLO & Error Budget: All SLOs from §2.2 with burn-rate trends
controlFields.Tenant Anomaly: Per-tenant traffic, isolation-denial events, quota usage
controlFields.DR Posture: Replication lag, last DR test, RTO/RPO tracking
controlFields.FinOps / Cost-to-Serve: Per-tenant + per-cell cost; cross-subsidy break-even (BR-005, R-001)
controlFields.DR Strategy: **Active-Passive within UK region** + **Cross-region warm-standby** for catastrophic primary-region loss
controlFields.Primary Region: UK-region selected per ADR-002 (e.g., AWS `eu-west-2` London / Azure UK South — actual selection in `/arckit:research`)
controlFields.DR Region: Second UK-eligible region OR multi-AZ within primary region for AZ-level events
controlFields.Replication: Postgres async replication < 5 min lag baseline (alerts at > 10 min); object-store cross-region replication; OTel backend cross-region
controlFields.Production wave-1 / wave-2: Wave-1 after 30-min canary soak; wave-2 after 1-h wave-1 soak
controlFields.Sovereign-bundle release: Project 002 liaison + Security Lead + Service Owner
controlFields.Deployment frequency: Daily to prod (per cell wave) at GA + 6 months

ARC-001-DIAG-001-v1.0.title: Architecture Diagram (C4 Model): ArcKit as a Service

ARC-001-DIAG-002-v1.0.title: Architecture Diagram: ArcKit SaaS — Critical Interaction Sequence Flows

controlFields.`tenant_id` propagation: Resolved from OIDC `tid` claim at the API Gateway; carried in request context, on every internal call (header `X-ArcKit-Tenant-Id` plus signed JWT), in every database query (row-level security), in every object-storage prefix, and on every AI-provider call. Default-deny if missing.
controlFields.Service-to-service mTLS: Every internal service-to-service call inside a cell uses mutual TLS via the service mesh; pod identities are SPIFFE-style; tokens are short-lived (<= 60 minutes for human; <= 5 minutes for service).
controlFields.Audit-log append: Every state-changing call emits an OpenTelemetry event tagged with `tenant_id`, actor identity, command, timestamp, request_id; the audit pipeline appends to a tamper-evident hash chain (each record's hash includes the prior record's hash). PII is redacted at source.
controlFields.Identity tokens: OIDC ID tokens for end users (vendor IdP for SMEs, federated for enterprise); WebAuthn hardware-key MFA for operators on a separate IdP; service tokens are workload-bound (mTLS + SPIFFE).
controlFields.Failure-safe defaults: Manual authoring (FR-003) always works even if AI is down; signup fails closed if Companies House is unreachable for verification; export fails closed (no partial archive).
